title: spec_driven_answer_spec_v1
description: spec_driven_v1 N1 Define Spec output. explicit_references = regulatory documents/clauses explicitly named in the query (literal preservation). required_slots = evidence pieces that support the answer (lexical keywords per slot). governing_normative_class = the authority class the answer is anchored on. answer_structure = a short description of the composition logic.

reasoning.description: 1-3 sentence rationale written *before* fixing the spec (the query's language is fine): which explicit references / authority class / slots you choose and why. Guided decoding decodes this field first, so the subsequent decision fields are conditioned on it (leading CoT).

facet.description: The *kind* of evidence this slot retrieves — a node in the licensing reasoning chain (a signal for query routing and answer rendering), NOT a value/conclusion. The chain (each layer lives in a different document family): requirement=the binding obligation (10 CFR/GDC/App); acceptance_criterion=the concrete pass threshold/method the staff applies (SRP for LWR, DSRS for NuScale); demonstration_method=*how* compliance was shown — analysis method, code (e.g. NRELAP5), assumptions, single-failure, conservatism (Topical Report/FSAR); applicant_design=the applicant's design assertion (FSAR/DCA); review_finding=the NRC staff's independent judgment/acceptance basis (SER/FSER); open_item_condition=remaining issues, imposed conditions, ITAAC, COL items, RAI resolutions (RAI/SER conditions); technical_basis=the *source/derivation/conservatism/validity-envelope/edition* behind a limit value (why that number — RG/Topical, not just the number); exemption_departure=GDC exemption / principal design criteria / departure and its staff disposition (SMR adaptation of LWR-era rules); applicability=reactor type/plant condition/licensing stage/effective edition the layer binds; definition=the defined regulatory meaning/scope of a term; cross_reference=another clause/appendix/table/companion document this one points to (also seeds follow-up search). Keep applicant_design and review_finding separate — never merge claim and finding.

expected_authority.description: Hint for which document family / authority holds this facet's evidence (e.g. 'binding 10 CFR', 'guidance RG/SRP/DSRS', 'applicant FSAR', 'review SER/RAI'). A label that helps the query node pick a collection filter/boost — never a value or conclusion.

topic_label.description: A short topic label (a few words) identifying the subject this query is about (e.g. 'ECCS acceptance criteria', 'seismic design'). Used only for multi-turn topic-shift detection across follow-up turns — a label, never a value or conclusion. Omit/null for one-off queries.
